purpose: Pin the cancellation-loss and orphan-task deltas that the Tokio migration proof pack must report before recommending a rewrite.
validation_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_asupersync_candeb cargo test -p asupersync --test tokio_migration_cancel_delta_contract --features test-internals -- --nocapture

deltas.tokio_reference_observation: A plain Tokio send future owns the value while waiting for capacity; dropping the future before completion can abandon the caller's value unless the caller uses an explicit reservation discipline.
deltas.asupersync_proof_row: channel::mpsc::Sender::reserve(&Cx).await returns a permit; the value is committed only by permit.send(value), so cancellation before commit leaves ownership with the caller and records no sent message.
deltas.caveat_text: Tokio's reserve API can be used safely; this delta targets migrations from direct send-in-select patterns, not disciplined reserve_owned usage.
deltas.safe_migration_rewrite: Replace send-in-select with reserve(&Cx).await followed by permit.send(value), and keep value ownership outside the cancelled wait until the permit commits.

deltas.tokio_reference_observation: tokio::select! drops losing futures when a timeout branch wins; cleanup is limited to drop semantics unless the program explicitly drives loser shutdown.
deltas.asupersync_proof_row: Scope::race cancels the losing branch and drains it through the cancellation protocol before surfacing the winner.
deltas.caveat_text: This is an intentional semantic difference: Tokio can be correct with explicit abort-and-join code, but the convention is outside select! itself.
deltas.safe_migration_rewrite: Model timeout races with asupersync race/timeout combinators or explicitly abort and join every Tokio loser before returning.

deltas.tokio_reference_observation: A detached Tokio JoinHandle can keep running after its logical parent exits unless the parent keeps and awaits or aborts the handle.
deltas.asupersync_proof_row: asupersync Cx::spawn and Cx::spawn_in bind the task to a region, and region close requires owned children to quiesce before close completes.
deltas.caveat_text: Tokio programs can enforce this with JoinSet or explicit handle management; this proof targets migrations from unstructured spawn ownership.
deltas.safe_migration_rewrite: Move unstructured tokio::spawn sites into Cx::spawn or Cx::spawn_in and close the owning region at shutdown boundaries.

deltas.tokio_reference_observation: Cancelled Tokio mutex or semaphore waiters must be reasoned about at each call site; queue position and held guard cleanup are separate convention-level responsibilities.
deltas.asupersync_proof_row: asupersync sync::Semaphore::acquire(&Cx, permits) and Cx-aware mutex waiters unlink cancelled waiters and preserve permit/guard accounting as part of the primitive contract.
deltas.caveat_text: This does not claim Tokio leaks permits on cancellation; it records that asupersync makes the obligation invariant explicit and reportable.
deltas.safe_migration_rewrite: Use asupersync sync primitives where cancellation is part of the wait contract, and keep held permits/guards inside region-owned cleanup scopes.
